mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates expanding liquidity and consumer purchasing power, which jointly produce positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5 percent annually while FEDFUNDS remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which directly supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 215.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside declining unemployment indicate synchronized strength in residential investment and labor demand, jointly forecasting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when HOUST growth exceeds 10 percent but UNRATE remains above 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent annually coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.
